_doc: Contact, certifications, and career history read by resume-tailoring (skill #13). This is the RICH schema the engine actually consumes (build_resume.py + build_cover_letter.py). Fill every bracketed [placeholder] with your real value during onboarding and keep the structure; add named roles, education entries, and credential variants as you need them. resume-tailoring refuses to run while any [bracket token] remains (the TD-051 placeholder guard), so a half-filled file fails loudly at load time instead of emitting a misattributed resume. Segments are ordered runs rendered in place: {"type":"text","text":"..."} for plain text, or {"type":"hyperlink","text":"...","url_key":"<key in cert_urls>"} for a clickable credential whose URL comes from cert_urls. The *_variants blocks let you keep more than one credential framing (for example a short and a long certifications line); keep the "default" entry and add named variants only if a per-application config selects them. For a complete, filled, fictional worked example, see resume-tailoring/applications/_synthetic/career/contact_block.json. No data here is shared outside your own PKS repo.

contact.website_display: [yoursite.com, or repeat your LinkedIn if you have no site]

segments.text: ) | [add or remove credentials here, or delete this whole variant if you have none]

tier_3.title: [Earlier job title, condensed to a single line]

education.capstone: [Capstone, honors, or a relevant-coursework line, or set to an empty string]

tech_proficiencies_default: [Tool | Tool | Tool: your technical proficiencies, pipe-separated]
languages_line: [English (native), other languages, or set to an empty string]
